mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack reduces wage growth and consumer spending power, depressing corporate earnings forecasts and producing negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and decreases in the consumer price index indicate strong real demand growth without inflationary pressure, boosting profit margins and driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL move in opposite directions, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns follow industrial production declines in over 65% of cases during recovery regimes, invalidating the risk-off transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.5 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and related supply chains, reducing capital expenditure forecasts and generating negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive when housing starts drop below the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power in tightening environments.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment indicates robust labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, which drives positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models, or forward returns are negative when both labor indicators improve simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, each quarter-point increase in the federal funds rate forces immediate portfolio rebalancing away from growth equities, producing negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following rate hikes in tightening regimes, or the 5-day return distribution shows no significant negative skew post-announcement.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms sustained economic expansion, validating corporate earnings trajectories and generating positive quarterly S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative quarterly returns dominate when GDP growth surpasses 2.0 percent, or the signal exhibits a Rank IC below 0.01 across all rolling evaluation windows.
